headline: International Schools Surge to 235 Raising Segregation Concerns
context: Malaysia now has 235 registered international schools as of 2026, up from 180 in 2020, with growing local enrollment.

cards.big: 235 international schools now enroll 128,000 students with 62% being Malaysian
cards.sub: Annual fees range from RM25,000 to RM120,000 per student

cards.big: MOE lifted the 40% Malaysian student cap for international schools in 2012
cards.sub: Policy shift accelerated local enrollment and new school establishment

cards.big: Parents cite safety, English proficiency, and smaller classes as top 3 reasons
cards.sub: Survey of 2,000 parents by GEMS Education conducted in 2025

cards.big: International school growth correlates with 15% enrollment decline in urban national schools
cards.sub: Middle-class flight risks creating socioeconomic segregation in education

cards.big: When families abandon national schools the remaining students bear the cost of neglect
Should international school access for Malaysians be restricted to protect national schools
